title: Berlin BER Airport Departure Guide - Quick Summary
items: **For most Berlin BER Airport departures, aim to be at the terminal about 2 hours before a simple Schengen flight.** Add more time for checked bags, long-haul flights, non-Schengen gates, families or paperwork.
**BER Airport is in fare zone C.** From central Berlin, most visitors need a Berlin ABC ticket unless their pass already includes zone C.
**The airport station is under Terminal 1 on level U2.** Terminal 2 is nearby on foot, but the walk still matters when you have luggage or a tight departure.
**BER security is not one single method.** T1 areas 1 and 5 plus T2 use CT scanners, while T1 areas 2 and 4 still use conventional lanes; pack so you can handle the stricter 100 ml process.

**Do not plan around BER Runway bookings.** BER discontinued time-slot booking for BER Runway from 26 May 2026; BER Biometrics is a separate optional smartdepart app flow for eligible passengers aged 16+.
**Power banks belong in hand baggage, not checked luggage.** Normal phone power banks are usually under 100 Wh, but large battery packs should be checked against airline rules before airport day.
